problem.title: Simplify Path
problem.description: Given a string path, which is an absolute path (starting with a slash '/') to a file or directory in a Unix-style file system, convert it to the simplified canonical path.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Stack
solutions.description: Split the path on '/' and use a stack to simulate directory navigation: skip empty parts and '.', pop the stack on '..', and push valid directory names. Joining the stack elements with '/' at the end produces the canonical path. The stack naturally handles the back-navigation semantics of '..' without needing to re-scan the string.
